name: Ruta de las Aves Trail
name_original: Ruta de las aves – circular desde Navalcán por el embalse
description: Hike the Ruta de las Aves Trail, a moderate 7.8-mile route through diverse landscapes and a birdwatching haven near Navalcán Reservoir.
region_intro: Hiking around Navalcán offers diverse landscapes, from the tranquil Navalcán Reservoir to the varied terrain shaped by the Guadyerbas River. The region features well-preserved Mediterranean forests, pastures, and wetlands, providing a range of natural environments for exploration. Hikers can expect paths through traditional pastoral lands and areas with moderate elevation changes. Best hiking trails around Navalcán The most popular hiking route is Route along the Río Arenal — circular by the Playa de Pelayos, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 35 minutes to complete, following the river and offering scenic views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ruta de los Pescadores (PRC-ASP-1) — circular desde Arenas de San Pedro, a moderate 9.3 miles (15.0 km) path. This route explores the historical fishermen's trail, providing insights into the region's heritage. Local hikers also love the Walk along the fishermen's route (PRC-ASP-1) — Arenas de San Pedro, a 7.3 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 41 minutes. Hiking around Navalcán is defined by Mediterranean forests, the Navalcán Reservoir, and diverse terrain including pastures and wetlands. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Navalcán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Navalcán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Navalcán?
A: Navalcán offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 40 routes available. These trails cater to different fitness levels, ranging from easy walks to more challenging ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Navalcán?
A: The best times for hiking in Navalcán are generally spring and autumn, when the weather is mild and pleasant. Winter can also be rewarding, especially for birdwatching enthusiasts, as species like the Common Crane are frequently observed around the Navalcán Reservoir during this season.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers?
A: Yes, Navalcán has several easy hiking options. There are 12 trails classified as easy, offering gentle terrain and shorter distances perfect for a relaxed stroll through the natural surroundings.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For those seeking a more strenuous adventure, Navalcán features 3 difficult hiking routes. These trails typically involve greater distances and more significant elevation changes, providing a good challenge for experienced hikers.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Navalcán?
A: Hikers in Navalcán will encounter diverse landscapes, including well-preserved Mediterranean forests, expansive pastures, and wetlands. The region is shaped by the tranquil Navalcán Reservoir and the Guadyerbas River, offering varied scenery from plains to moderate mountain terrain. During dry periods, an ancient dolmen from the 4th millennium BC can even become visible in the reservoir.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes in Navalcán?
A: Yes, Navalcán offers several excellent circular routes. One popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318188689/ruta-de-los-pescadores-prc-asp-1-circular-desde-arenas-de-san-pedro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruta de los Pescadores (PRC-ASP-1) — circular desde Arenas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.3-mile (15.0 km) path that explores the region's historical heritage. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1318168693/santuario-de-san-pedro-de-alcantara-circular-desde-arenas-de-san-pedro-por-gr-180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santuario de San Pedro de Alcántara — circular desde Arenas de San Pedro por GR 180&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 6.2 miles (10.0 km) long.

Q: What historical or cultural sights can I find along the hiking trails?
A: The area around Navalcán is rich in history. Hikers can discover remnants like Roman bridges crossing the Porquerizo and La Pasadera streams, and the hydraulic complex of Montoya's mills. You might also encounter the ancient dolmen in the Navalcán Reservoir during periods of low water. Notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5678370" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aquelcabos Medieval Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2840991" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro de Alcántara Sanctuary&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any good spots for birdwatching while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The Navalcán Reservoir is a significant birdwatching spot, particularly for larger raptors and migratory birds. You can often observe species such as the Eurasian Black Vulture and the Spanish Imperial Eagle, especially young birds in spring. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6124905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching Hide at Tiétar River and Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a great spot to visit.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Navalcán?
A: The trails in Navalcán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 100 reviews. Hikers often praise the well-preserved natural environment, the diverse terrain, and the peaceful atmosphere, especially around the Navalcán Reservoir.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by water features or lakes?
A: Many routes in Navalcán feature water elements. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144065108/route-along-the-rio-arenal-circular-by-the-playa-de-pelayos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route along the Río Arenal — circular by the Playa de Pelayos&lt;/a&gt; follows the river and offers scenic views. The region is also home to the prominent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3166729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navalcán Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6089869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Cuevas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, both offering beautiful waterside paths.

Q: Are there any castles or ancient structures to explore nearby?
A: Yes, the area around Navalcán has several historical castles. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3986954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Arenas de San Pedro&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6297961" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Constable Dávalos (Castle of the Sad Countess)&lt;/a&gt;, which add a cultural dimension to your outdoor exploration.
tips: The marked itinerary of the bird route runs for a large part of its journey along the shore of the Navalcán reservoir, offering wonderful views that deserve a postcard.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Ruta de las Aves Trail is approximately 12.5 to 13.5 kilometers (7.8 to 8.4 miles) long. Most hikers complete it in about 3.5 hours, though komoot estimates it can be done in around 3 hours and 17 minutes.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ruta de las Aves Trail is generally considered easy and suitable for a wide range of hikers, including beginners and families. It has a mostly flat profile with minimal strenuous climbs. However, some sections may require careful footing due to granite rocks and dense vegetation.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ruta de las Aves Trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. You'll find wide, unpaved paths through private estates like 'La Dehesa', but also segments with abundant granite rock and dense vegetation where the path may be less defined. Some parts involve a slight ascent on stony ground as it moves away from the reservoir and into the monte. Expect a mix of dehesas, pine forests, and cork oak areas.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail is named 'Ruta de las Aves' (Bird Route) for a reason! It's excellent for birdwatching, especially around the Navalcán Reservoir, a designated Wildlife Refuge and Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA). You might spot diverse aquatic birds, endangered Iberian species like the Bonelli's eagle and booted eagle, and hear cranes. The flora includes Mediterranean species such as holm oaks, retama, cantueso, wild olive trees, and junipers. You'll also likely see various breeds of cattle in the dehesa areas.

faqs.question: What are the main scenic highlights along the way?
faqs.answer: Hikers can enjoy expansive views of the Navalcán Reservoir, which the trail borders. On clear days, you'll also get impressive views of the Sierra de Gredos, with Almanzor Peak visible in the distance. The diverse landscapes of dehesas, pine forests, and cork oaks provide beautiful scenery throughout.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to hike the Ruta de las Aves Trail are during autumn, winter, and spring. It is strongly recommended to avoid hiking in summer due to the extreme temperatures in the region, despite some shaded areas along the route.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Navalcán. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, it's advisable to look for parking within Navalcán itself, near the official start point of the PR-TO 13 route. As it's a circular trail, you'll return to your starting point.

faqs.question: Is the Ruta de las Aves Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas and private estates where cattle graze, dogs are generally permitted. However, it's crucial to keep your dog on a leash at all times, especially when near livestock and in areas designated for wildlife protection like the Navalcán Reservoir ZEPA, to ensure the safety of both your pet and the local fauna.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a permit or have to pay an entrance fee to hike the Ruta de las Aves Trail. It is part of the 'Senderos de Toledo' project, promoting public access to local heritage. However, please respect the natural environment, especially as it's within the Navalcán Reservoir Special Protection Area for Birds (ZEPA).

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ruta de las Aves Trail (PR-TO 13) is an official route that covers approximately 95% of the path known simply as "Ruta de las Aves" on OpenStreetMap. It is part of the broader 'Senderos de Toledo' network, which aims to highlight the region's cultural and environmental heritage.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for less defined paths, sturdy hiking shoes are recommended. Bring plenty of water, especially outside of cooler months, as there are no water sources directly on the trail. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also advisable due to open sections. Binoculars would be great for birdwatching!
